message:
fix(btree,pager,vdbe,types): harden cursor, overflow, record parsing, and error propagation

btree/cursor:
- Remove last_insert_was_rightmost_append tracking — the sequential-append
  fast path was unreliable after tree balancing and added unnecessary state
- Add MAX_OVERFLOW_CHAIN guard to prevent unbounded overflow page allocation
- Fix proptest: duplicate rowid test now directly calls table_insert instead
  of relying on table_move_to + is_found (which could miss the violation)
- Fix large-payload split test: track actual inserts_done for accurate
  counter assertion when loop breaks early on root split
- Increase prefetch delete test from 5000 to 10000 rows for better coverage
- Fix sequential insert test: use rowid (not max_rowid) in table_insert call

btree/freelist:
- Use saturating_sub instead of raw division-minus to prevent underflow
  in max_leaf_entries when usable_size is very small

core/remote_effects:
- Pass admission_now() to bulkhead.cancel_entry() to match updated API

pager/page_cache:
- Add short-read validation to read_db_header — return DatabaseCorrupt
  error if fewer than 100 bytes are read instead of silently using
  partial/zero data

types/record:
- Replace unwrap_or(u32::MAX) with ok()? in parse_record_header_into
  so that overflowing body_offset/value_len returns None instead of
  producing corrupt column offsets
- Add checked_add for end offset calculation in decode_column_from_offset
- Fix parse_record_prefix: empty data now returns None (corrupt) instead
  of Some(empty vec) — zero-length records are invalid per SQLite format

vdbe/engine:
- Propagate parse_record_into failures as proper errors (malformed probe
  key, malformed payload) instead of silently ignoring parse failures
  in index seek and >64-column eager decode paths

beads: update issue metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
